webforge.description: WebForge — AI agent organization that builds and maintains websites

resume.description: Resume a WebForge session — read all memory, rules, and where we stopped

init.description: Initialize WebForge in the current project

on.description: Turn WebForge ON for current project

off.description: Turn WebForge OFF for current project (saves session)

status.description: Check WebForge status for current project

rules.description: List all rules (do's and don'ts) for this project

add-rule.description: Add a new rule. Usage: /add-rule <rule text>

add-adr.template:
WEBFORGE_PROJECT="$OPENCODE_CWD" python3 -c "
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '$HOME/webforge/mcp')
from memory import add_adr
import os
os.environ['WEBFORGE_PROJECT'] = os.environ.get('OPENCODE_CWD', '.')
parts = '$1'.split('|')
if len(parts) >= 3:
    print(add_adr(parts[0].strip(), parts[1].strip(), parts[2].strip(), parts[3].strip() if len(parts) > 3 else '').to_dict())
else:
    print('Usage: /add-adr <title> | <context> | <decision> | <consequences>')
"

session-log.description: Read the last 7 days of session logs

stop.description: Mark end of session — where you stopped, what's next. Usage: /stop <summary>

probe.description: Probe the current project (auto-detects fresh vs existing). Scans codebase, identifies what's decided/missing.

probe-existing.description: Probe an EXISTING project — scans codebase without asking questions. Use for projects that already have code.

probe-fresh.description: Probe a FRESH project — asks questions. Use for new projects with no code yet.

scan.description: Scan the project and print a complete map (pages, components, API routes, ADRs, tests, git info).

correct.description: Turn a correction into a permanent rule. Add | global to save for all projects. Usage: /correct <what was wrong> | <what to do instead> [| global]

review.description: Meta Engineering review — scans session for corrections, proposes rules. Run at end of session.

meta-status.description: Show what Meta Engineering has learned (rules, corrections, system memory).

build.description: Start the Build phase — wakes Hephaestus and the Build Team to start building.

task.description: Create a new task in the backlog. Usage: /task <title> [type] [area] [effort]

tasks.description: Show the Kanban board (Backlog, TODO, DOING, DONE, Blocked).

task-show.description: Show details of a specific task. Usage: /task-show <task-id>

task-move.description: Move a task to a different column. Usage: /task-move <id> <backlog|todo|doing|done|blocked>

task-done.description: Mark a task as done. Usage: /task-done <id> [summary]

task-block.description: Mark a task as blocked. Usage: /task-block <id> <reason>

task-approve.description: Approve the proposed task and start working on it. Usage: /task-approve <id> [agent]

task-reject.description: Reject the proposed task, move back to backlog, propose next. Usage: /task-reject <id> [reason]

build.description: PROPOSAL GATE: Shows the next task to work on and waits for your approval. You are the first point of contact before any Hephaestus agent starts.

rfc.description: Show the RFC (design proposal) for a task. Usage: /rfc <task-id>

rfc-approve.description: Approve the RFC for a task — unlocks coding. Usage: /rfc-approve <task-id>

rfc-reject.description: Reject the RFC — sends task back to backlog. Usage: /rfc-reject <task-id> [reason]

rfcs.description: List all RFCs and their statuses.

check.description: Run quality checks (lint, type-check, tests, build, security). Blocks task-done if failed. Usage: /check [task-id]

check-approve.description: Override failed quality checks. 'I know about the warning, mark it done anyway.' Usage: /check-approve <task-id> [reason]

bug.description: Report a bug — creates a bugfix task in the Kanban board. Usage: /bug <description> [severity]

review.description: Generate a code review checklist for a task. Usage: /review <task-id>

readme.description: Generate README from project state. Output to .webforge/docs/ for review.

changelog.description: Generate changelog from git history (Keep a Changelog format).

api-docs.description: Generate API documentation from route files.

onboard.description: Generate onboarding document for new developers/sessions. Pulls from ALL sources.

docs.description: Generate ALL documentation (README, changelog, API docs, env docs, onboarding) at once.

standup.description: 🏃 Daily standup — what we did, what we're doing, what's blocked. Suggested next action.

escalate.description: 📤 Escalate a question to the developer (CEO). Law 5: No Inference. Usage: /escalate <question>

answer.description: ✅ Answer an escalated question. Usage: /answer <escalation-id> <your answer>

escalations.description: List all open escalations (questions waiting for your answer).

agents.description: 🤖 List all available agents you can talk to. Use /talk or @mention to address them directly.

talk.description: 📤 Talk directly to a specific agent. Usage: /talk <agent-name> <message>. Example: /talk Hermes what should I work on next?

notifications.description: 📬 Show all unread notifications across all agents (the phone system).

inbox.description: 📬 Show an agent's notification inbox. Usage: /inbox <agent-name>

read-notifications.description: ✅ Mark an agent's notifications as read. Usage: /read-notifications <agent-name>

dispatch.description: 📬 Show routing status for a task. Usage: /dispatch <task-id>

dispatch-route.description: 📬 Route a task to its department head. Usage: /dispatch-route <task-id>

dispatch-down.description: 📬 Route a task DOWN to a specific agent. Usage: /dispatch-down <task-id> <agent-name>

dispatch-up.description: 📬 Route results UP the chain. Usage: /dispatch-up <task-id> <from-agent>

dispatch-chain.description: 🔗 Show chain of command for an agent. Usage: /dispatch-chain [agent-name]

dispatch-pending.description: 📬 Show all tasks that haven't been routed yet.
